data.title: Crypto Trader 'Maji' Increases Long Positions Amid Market Fluctuations
data.description:
On April 11, 'Maji' continued to strategically increase long positions, according to BlockBeats. The total value of these positions has risen to $42.17 million, up from approximately $34 million the previous evening. These positions are reported to have a high safety margin, with liquidation prices significantly lower than current market values.

As of the latest update, 'Maji' holds detailed positions including a 25x leverage on Ethereum with 12,131.7179 ETH at a liquidation price of $2,090.95, a 40x leverage on Bitcoin with 180 BTC at a liquidation price of $62,529.3, and a 10x leverage on HYPE with 43,750 units.

Additionally, 'Maji' has achieved a total contract profit of $1.85 million over the past week, and has turned a monthly loss into a profit of $190,000.

data.title: A whale withdrew 265,100 LINK tokens from Binance, worth $2.38 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, three hours ago, a whale withdrew 265,100 LINK tokens from Binance, worth $2.38 million.

data.title: Joe Lubin Advocates for Ethereum-Based Strategy Model
data.description: Joe Lubin has proposed that an Ethereum version of the Strategy model could be more effective than merely holding Bitcoin. According to NS3.AI, Lubin suggests that this approach would involve continuously accumulating and staking ETH, with all funds allocated to ETH from the outset to maximize compounded returns.

data.title: U.S. Agrees to Unfreeze Iranian Assets in Foreign Banks
data.description: The United States has agreed to lift the freeze on Iranian assets held in banks in Qatar and other foreign countries. According to BlockBeats, this development was confirmed by a senior Iranian official. The decision marks a significant step in the ongoing negotiations between the two nations. Further details on the implementation of this agreement have not been disclosed.

data.title: Ethereum co-founder Joe Lubin: The "Ethereum version of the Strategy model" can be done better.
data.description: Bitmine Chairman Tom Lee shared an interview video of Ethereum co-founder Joe Lubin on the X platform. Lubin stated that after meeting with Michael Saylor, founder of the Bitcoin treasury company Strategy, he discovered that the "Ethereum version of the Strategy model" could be done even better. Using Bitmine and Tom Lee's model as examples, Lubin explained that by continuously accumulating and staking ETH, the core strategy is to allocate 100% of funds to ETH and participate in staking to generate returns, achieving compound growth from day one. Compared to simply holding Bitcoin, Ethereum can achieve asset appreciation and reallocation through staking returns and a multi-layered reward mechanism within its ecosystem, driving the balance sheet to shift from a "holding-oriented" to a "return + allocation-oriented" model.

data.title: TD Cowen Analyst Lowers Target Price for MicroStrategy
data.description: TD Cowen analyst Lance Vitanza has revised the target price for Bitcoin reserve company MicroStrategy (MSTR) from $440 to $350, while maintaining a buy rating. According to Odaily, this adjustment reflects the analyst's updated assessment of the company's financial outlook.

data.title: Sources say the United States has agreed to lift the freeze on Iranian assets held in Qatar and other foreign banks.
data.description: According to Reuters, a senior Iranian source said the United States has agreed to lift the freeze on Iranian assets held in Qatar and other foreign banks.

data.title: TD Cowen analysts lowered their price target for MSTR to $350 but maintained a buy rating.
data.description: TD Cowen, an investment bank with $15 billion in assets under management, has lowered its price target for Bitcoin Reserve Strategy MSTR from $440 to $350, while maintaining a buy rating. (BitcoinTreasuries.NET)

data.title: Iran's Vice President Comments on Potential U.S. Negotiations
data.description: On April 11, Iran's First Vice President Aref expressed on social media that negotiations between Iran and U.S. representatives in Islamabad, Pakistan, could lead to a mutually beneficial agreement. According to BlockBeats, Aref noted that if the U.S. representatives prioritize 'America First,' the chances of reaching a favorable agreement increase. However, if the representatives prioritize 'Israel First,' no agreement will be achieved, and Iran will continue to defend itself more resolutely than before, potentially leading to greater global consequences.

data.title: Grayscale has updated its list of future investment considerations, adding assets such as Hype and Virtual.
data.description: Grayscale has announced an update to its list of crypto assets it plans to consider including in future investment products. The latest list includes Bitcoin and XRP in the Currencies category; Stacks, Sui, Toncoin, and Tron in the Smart Contract Platforms category; Ethena, Hyperliquid, and Jupiter in the Financials category; Dogecoin and Decentraland in the Consumer & Culture category; Worldcoin, Virtuals Protocol, Story, and Grass in the AI ​​category; and Chainlink, LayerZero, and Helium in the Utilities & Services category. Grayscale stated that the list is evaluated quarterly to provide investors with diversified digital asset investment options.

data.title: Iran's First Vice President: If the US represents "Israel First," negotiations will end in failure.
data.description: Iranian First Vice President Arik Shakhtar Alev posted on social media on the 11th that if Iran negotiates with the "America First" US representatives in Islamabad, Pakistan, it is likely to reach an agreement beneficial to both sides and the world. However, if facing the "Israel First" US representatives, no agreement will be reached, and "we will inevitably continue to defend ourselves more resolutely than ever before, and the world will pay a greater price." (Xinhua)

data.title: US media: Vance played a key role in facilitating US-Iran negotiations
data.description: According to a CNN report on the 11th, a senior Pakistani source revealed that US Vice President Vance played a key role in facilitating the US-Iran talks to begin in Islamabad, Pakistan. The source, familiar with the negotiations, stated that they estimate it will take several days to finalize a ceasefire agreement, and Pakistani officials hope to persuade Vance to extend his stay in Pakistan to help facilitate this. Furthermore, a senior Gulf region source revealed that Iran has extremely low trust in US President Trump's special envoy, Witkov. Previously, Witkov had led several rounds of negotiations with Iran, but these were all interrupted by the US military strikes against Iran. Witkov is one of the members of the US delegation participating in the Islamabad talks. (China News Service)

data.title: South Korea Considers Seizing Principal in Virtual Asset Insider Trading Cases
data.description:
South Korea's financial regulators are contemplating the inclusion of a clause to seize the principal in virtual asset insider trading cases in the upcoming second phase of legislation expected later this year. According to PANews, under the current Virtual Asset User Protection Act, the government can only confiscate investment principal in cases of fraudulent transactions or market manipulation. There is no legal basis for seizing the principal in insider trading cases.

The Financial Supervisory Service has recently submitted this issue to the Financial Services Commission, which is currently reviewing whether to incorporate it into the second phase of the law. In the stock market, all improper trading crimes can result in the confiscation of the investment principal.

data.title: Rory McIlroy's Chances to Win 2026 US Masters Surge to 70% on Polymarket
data.description: According to NS3.AI, the probability of Rory McIlroy winning the 2026 US Masters has increased significantly on Polymarket, reaching 70% after a 46% rise within 24 hours. The trading volume for the 2026 US Masters Champion contract has surpassed $107 million, indicating strong investor interest and activity.

data.title: Polymarket's odds of "Rory McIlroy winning the 2026 Masters Tournament" have risen to 70%, a 46% increase in the last 24 hours.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the probability of Rory McIlroy winning the 2026 Masters Tournament on Polymarket has risen to 70%, a 46% increase in 24 hours. As of now, the total value of contracts for the "2026 Masters Tournament Winner" event has exceeded $107 million. The rules for this event contract are as follows: settlement is based on the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament. If a player is eliminated according to the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will be settled as "No." In the event of a tie, settlement will be based on the winner determined by the official rules of the Masters Tournament. If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, the settlement will be "Other." The settlement is primarily based on the official results published on the PGA Tour website and the Masters Tournament website, and may also consider consensus from other reliable reports. The 90th Masters Tournament 2026 officially teed off this week at Augusta National Golf Club. Defending champion Rory McIlroy shot a 67, tying for the lead with American Sam Burns after the first round. Chinese player Li Haotong carded a 71, tied for 10th. After the first day of competition, Australian Jason Day, Japanese-American Kurt Kitayama, and 2018 Masters champion Patrick Reed are all tied for third with 69s. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: The grayscale update is considering including product tokens, adding assets such as HYPE, STX, and VIRTUAL.

The latest list includes Bitcoin and XRP in the Currencies category; Stacks, Sui, Toncoin, and Tron in the Smart Contract Platforms category; Ethena, Hyperliquid, and Jupiter in the Financials category; Dogecoin and Decentraland in the Consumer & Culture category; Worldcoin, Virtuals Protocol, Story, and Grass in the AI ​​category; and Chainlink, LayerZero, and Helium in the Utilities & Services category. Grayscale stated that the list is evaluated quarterly to provide investors with diversified digital asset investment options.

data.title: Kamala Harris Considers 2028 Presidential Run
data.description: Kamala Harris has indicated that she might run for president in 2028. According to NS3.AI, Harris made this comment during the National Action Network conference held in New York City. Her statement has sparked discussions about her potential candidacy in the upcoming presidential election.

data.title: Harris said she is "considering" running for the 2028 US presidential election.
data.description: According to NBC News, at the National Action Network conference in New York City, former Vice President Kamala Harris, when asked about running for president in 2028, said, "I might. I'm thinking about it." This is Harris's strongest and most direct statement on the question of whether she is considering running again. (Jinshi)

data.title: Putin: Russia needs to develop its own large-scale language model to safeguard the country's development process.
data.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin stated on the 10th, while attending a government meeting on the development of artificial intelligence technology, that large language models have become a fundamental, end-to-end technology for development across all sectors of the country. He pointed out, "Only by possessing our own independent models can we confidently move forward, ensure security and defense capabilities, and remain at the forefront of scientific and engineering thought. Moreover, Russia is one of the few countries in the world with unique expertise in this field." Putin also mentioned that so-called open-source solutions are often used to develop language models. He explained, "Developers from various countries share complete algorithm libraries, and the resources of this cooperation and partnership should naturally be effectively utilized." (Jin Shi)
